---
format:
  html:
    page-layout: custom
    theme: none
    document-css: false
    toc: false
    anchor-sections: false
    embed-resources: true
    css: pitch.css
title: "Polars Rollup Pipeline — one process, one pass"
---

<!-- ══════════════ SLIDE 1 ══════════════ -->
::: {#s1 .slide}

[01 / 06]{.slide-number}

[Pipeline comparison]{.eyebrow}

## Architecture

::: {.subtitle}
Compute blended forecast EP curves from the RiskLink and Verisk YLTs,
fan them out to the 21 Hisco tables. 

Different mechanics.
:::

|                               | January DuckDB                    | dbt (finalised)                    | Polars                                  |
|-------------------------------|-----------------------------------|------------------------------------|----------------------------------------|
| Runtime model                 | DuckDB in process views    | Duckdb with dbt      | Single Python process                   |
| Infrastructure                | Local DuckDB on a file server     | dbt + DuckDB + dbt cli         | Python + polars.             |
| Schema safety at boundaries   | [Silent drift]{.no}               | [Silent drift]{.no}                | [Enforced per frame]{.yes}              |
| Pre-flight data-quality check | [None]{.no}                       | [`dbt test` (after the run)]{.no}  | [Interactive CLI, before the run]{.yes} |
| Iteration loop                | Re-run the notebook, wait         | Compile → dispatch → wait          | `pytest` in 1.4 s                       |
| Logic footprint               | 60+ SQL views, ~2 000 LOC         | ~600 lines SQL + Jinja             | ~350 lines of typed Python              |
| Performance characteristics   | No materialized views done manually         | Dbt controlls materialization | In memory caching and automatic query optimizer|

> No untyped sql, no jinja macros. 

:::


<!-- ══════════════ SLIDE 2 ══════════════ -->
::: {#s2 .slide}

[02 / 06]{.slide-number}

[Correctness]{.eyebrow}

## Fail fast, [not three joins later.]{.accent}

::: {.subtitle}
Typed Python.
Every column is an enum. Every frame has a declared schema.
Every stage validates its inputs and outputs at the boundary.
Schema drift blows up the pipeline where it happened.
:::

:::: {.code-grid}
::: {.code-card}
[Column names as enum]{.card-label}

```python
class RefLobsCol(StrEnum):
    LOB_ID             = "lob_id"
    MODELLED_LOB       = "modelled_lob"
    ROLLUP_LOB         = "rollup_lob"
    LOB_TYPE           = "lob_type"
    CDS_CAT_CLASS_NAME = "cds_cat_class_name"
    OFFICE             = "office"
    CLASS              = "class"
```
:::

::: {.code-card}
[Typed schema per frame]{.card-label}

```python
REF_LOBS: pl.Schema = pl.Schema({
    RefLobsCol.LOB_ID:             pl.Int64,
    RefLobsCol.MODELLED_LOB:       pl.String,
    RefLobsCol.ROLLUP_LOB:         pl.String,
    RefLobsCol.LOB_TYPE:           pl.String,
    RefLobsCol.CDS_CAT_CLASS_NAME: pl.String,
    RefLobsCol.OFFICE:             pl.String,
    RefLobsCol.CLASS:              pl.String,
})
```
:::
::::

1. **Typos don't compile.** Column names are Python enum members, not strings scattered across SQL files.
2. **Schema drift surfaces at the boundary.** `validate_schema()` runs on every stage's inputs and outputs — errors name the frame, the missing columns, and the dtype mismatches.
3. **Seeds are schema-checked at load.** A renamed column in a CSV stops the pipeline before any math runs.
4. **57 unit tests, 1.4 s.** The whole contract is exercised on every change, not just in production.

> If it's wrong, it fails loudly, at the line where it became wrong.

:::


<!-- ══════════════ SLIDE 3 ══════════════ -->
::: {#s3 .slide}

[03 / 06]{.slide-number}

[Ergonomics]{.eyebrow}

## Gain confidence [before it starts.]{.accent}

::: {.subtitle}
One command inspects every file the pipeline will touch, validates its
schema, and reports status in plain language. Then it asks permission.
:::

```{=html}
<div class="terminal-wrap"><div class="terminal">
<div class="terminal-chrome">
  <span class="tl-dots"><span class="tl-dot r"></span><span class="tl-dot y"></span><span class="tl-dot g"></span></span>
  <span class="tl-title">polars — rollup pipeline</span>
</div>
<div class="terminal-body"><span class="t-prompt">$</span> <span class="t-cmd">python -m rollup.pipeline --dry-run</span>

<span class="t-hdr">[seeds]</span>  <span class="t-path">polars/seeds</span>
  <span class="t-ok">✓</span> lobs                            <span class="t-num">     62</span> rows   <span class="t-dim">schema OK</span>
  <span class="t-ok">✓</span> blending_factors                <span class="t-num">     30</span> rows   <span class="t-dim">schema OK</span>
  <span class="t-ok">✓</span> forecast_factors                <span class="t-num">     78</span> rows   <span class="t-dim">schema OK</span>
  <span class="t-ok">✓</span> fx_rates                        <span class="t-num">      6</span> rows   <span class="t-dim">schema OK</span>
  <span class="t-ok">✓</span> euws_rate_factors               <span class="t-num"> 69,212</span> rows   <span class="t-dim">schema OK</span>
  <span class="t-ok">✓</span> dim_region_perils               <span class="t-num">  2,138</span> rows   <span class="t-dim">schema OK</span>
  <span class="t-ok">✓</span> dim_risklink_analysis           <span class="t-num">  1,042</span> rows   <span class="t-dim">schema OK</span>
  <span class="t-ok">✓</span> air_events                      <span class="t-num">410,024</span> rows   <span class="t-dim">schema OK</span>
  <span class="t-ok">✓</span> fineart_adjustments             <span class="t-num">    186</span> rows   <span class="t-dim">schema OK</span>

<span class="t-hdr">[ylt verisk]</span>    <span class="t-path">data/ylt/verisk/</span>    <span class="t-dim">air_ylt_*.parquet</span>       <span class="t-dim">n_simulations=</span><span class="t-num">10,000</span>
  <span class="t-ok">✓</span> air_ylt_c1.parquet                       <span class="t-num">148.3 MB</span>
  <span class="t-ok">✓</span> air_ylt_c2.parquet                       <span class="t-num">102.5 MB</span>

<span class="t-hdr">[ylt risklink]</span>  <span class="t-path">data/ylt/risklink/</span>  <span class="t-dim">risklink_ylt_*.parquet</span>  <span class="t-dim">n_simulations=</span><span class="t-num">100,000</span>
  <span class="t-ok">✓</span> risklink_ylt_01.parquet                    <span class="t-num">2.1 GB</span>

<span class="t-dim">Seeds:</span> <span class="t-ok">11/11</span> <span class="t-dim">valid.</span>   <span class="t-dim">YLTs:</span> <span class="t-ok">2/2</span> <span class="t-dim">vendors.</span>   <span class="t-dim">EP summaries:</span> <span class="t-ok">2/2</span> <span class="t-dim">vendors.</span>

<span class="t-ans">Proceed? [y/N]:</span> <span class="caret"></span></div>
</div></div>
```

> No surprises at row 3 million. Every input is accounted for on line one.

:::


<!-- ══════════════ SLIDE 4 ══════════════ -->
::: {#s4 .slide}

[04 / 06]{.slide-number}

[Speed]{.eyebrow}

## Minutes becomes [seconds.]{.accent}

::: {.subtitle}
Measured on the production-sized input — 4.4M-row Verisk YLT plus the
full RiskLink YLT. Full chain, all 21 Hisco tables written.
:::

```{=html}
<div class="speed">
<div class="speed-row">
<div class="speed-label">Pre-January SQL Server<span class="sub">bespoke warehouse queries, manual extract</span></div>
<div class="speed-bar-track"><div class="speed-bar sql-server">unknown — likely hours</div></div>
<div class="speed-time">unknown · &gt; 1–2 hrs</div>
</div>
<div class="speed-row">
<div class="speed-label">January DuckDB<span class="sub">local filesystem, 60+ views</span></div>
<div class="speed-bar-track"><div class="speed-bar january">2 – 20 minutes</div></div>
<div class="speed-time">dinosaur-bound</div>
</div>
<div class="speed-row">
<div class="speed-label">dbt (finalised)<span class="sub">warehouse round-trip, per-model materialize</span></div>
<div class="speed-bar-track"><div class="speed-bar dbt">longer than DuckDB, always</div></div>
<div class="speed-time">compile + dispatch tax</div>
</div>
<div class="speed-row">
<div class="speed-label">Polars lazy pipeline<span class="sub">single process, one optimisation pass</span></div>
<div class="speed-bar-track"><div class="speed-bar polars">~3s</div></div>
<div class="speed-time fast">a couple of seconds</div>
</div>
</div>
<div class="why-grid">
<div class="why">
<div class="n">01</div>
<h4>One collect, 21 sinks</h4>
<p>The whole lazy graph is optimised once; shared work is computed once.</p>
</div>
<div class="why">
<div class="n">02</div>
<h4>Cached DAG node</h4>
<p>One-line <code>.cache()</code> replaces the DuckDB materialised-table trick.</p>
</div>
<div class="why">
<div class="n">03</div>
<h4>No SQL compile. No Jinja.</h4>
<p>Parquet in, parquet out. No warehouse dispatch, no network round-trip.</p>
</div>
<div class="why">
<div class="n">04</div>
<h4>Zero copies</h4>
<p>Polars operates on Arrow buffers end-to-end. No serialisation between steps.</p>
</div>
</div>
```

> Change a blending weight. Re-run.

:::


<!-- ══════════════ SLIDE 5 ══════════════ -->
::: {#s5 .slide}

[05 / 06]{.slide-number}

[Future]{.eyebrow}

## Integrate with [Validnator]{.accent}

::: {.subtitle}
Schema validation catches the wrong shape. Validnator catches the wrong values —
FK integrity, range bounds, uniqueness, cross-column constraints — all declared in YAML,
all checked before the pipeline touches a single loss row.
:::

:::: {.future-grid}
::: {.future-col}
[Today — shape only]{.card-label}

| Check | Caught by |
|---|---|
| Missing column | `validate_schema()` |
| Wrong dtype | `validate_schema()` |
| Extra column | `validate_schema()` |
| Value out of range | [Not checked]{.no} |
| Broken FK (`peril_id` unknown) | [Not checked]{.no} |
| Weights don't sum to 1.0 | [Not checked]{.no} |
| Duplicate key | [Not checked]{.no} |

:::
::: {.future-col}
[With validnator — values too]{.card-label}

```yaml
rules:
  - code: W01
    column: weight
    check: valid_range
    params: {min: 0.0, max: 1.0}

  - code: E01
    column: peril_id
    check: referential_integrity
    params: {reference: seeds/perils.csv,
             reference_column: peril_id}

  - code: E02
    columns: [peril_id, vendor]
    check: is_unique_composite

  - code: E03
    column: weight
    check: sum
    params: {expected: 1.0, group_by: peril_id}
```

:::
::::

> Run validnator on every seed CSV in CI. Broken data never reaches the pipeline.

:::


<!-- ══════════════ SLIDE 6 ══════════════ -->
::: {#s6 .slide}

[06 / 06]{.slide-number}

[Scope]{.eyebrow}

## What this pipeline [does not do.]{.accent}

::: {.subtitle}
The pipeline owns the blending and the EP maths. These four jobs sit
outside that boundary — they are inputs or downstream consumers, not
steps in the chain.
:::

```{=html}
<div class="out-of-scope">

<div class="oos-row">
  <div class="oos-n">01</div>
  <div class="oos-body">
    <h4>Extract RDM results</h4>
    <p>RDM output must be pulled and converted to Parquet before the pipeline starts.
    The pipeline reads YLTs; it does not connect to RDM or trigger any model run.</p>
  </div>
</div>

<div class="oos-row">
  <div class="oos-n">02</div>
  <div class="oos-body">
    <h4>Produce clean summary EP curves</h4>
    <p>The pipeline writes per-event loss rows to the 21 Hisco parquets.
    Aggregating those into summary EP curves for consumption (charts, reports) is a
    separate downstream step.</p>
  </div>
</div>

<div class="oos-row">
  <div class="oos-n">03</div>
  <div class="oos-body">
    <h4>Run the RiskLink simulation</h4>
    <p>RiskLink analysis IDs are declared in <code>seeds/analyses.csv</code>.
    The pipeline expects the corresponding YLT parquets to already exist on disk —
    it does not invoke RiskLink or manage any simulation jobs.</p>
  </div>
</div>

<div class="oos-row">
  <div class="oos-n">04</div>
  <div class="oos-body">
    <h4>Push results to CDS automatically</h4>
    <p>Hisco parquets are written locally. Uploading them to CDS is a manual step
    today — or a thin wrapper around the pipeline, not part of the pipeline itself.</p>
  </div>
</div>

</div>
```

> All can be extended because rollup is a self contained pipeline of queries

:::


<!-- ══════════════ NAV ══════════════ -->
<nav class="progress" aria-label="Slide navigation">
  <a href="#s1" class="active" aria-label="Slide 1"></a>
  <a href="#s2" aria-label="Slide 2"></a>
  <a href="#s3" aria-label="Slide 3"></a>
  <a href="#s4" aria-label="Slide 4"></a>
  <a href="#s5" aria-label="Slide 5"></a>
  <a href="#s6" aria-label="Slide 6"></a>
</nav>

<div class="hint"><kbd>←</kbd> <kbd>→</kbd> <kbd>space</kbd> to navigate</div>

<script>
const slides = document.querySelectorAll('.slide');
const dots   = document.querySelectorAll('.progress a');

const io = new IntersectionObserver((entries) => {
  entries.forEach(e => {
    if (e.isIntersecting) {
      const i = Array.from(slides).indexOf(e.target);
      dots.forEach((d, j) => d.classList.toggle('active', i === j));
    }
  });
}, { threshold: 0.55 });

slides.forEach(s => io.observe(s));

function currentIndex() {
  const mid = window.scrollY + window.innerHeight / 2;
  for (let i = 0; i < slides.length; i++) {
    const top = slides[i].offsetTop;
    const bot = top + slides[i].offsetHeight;
    if (mid >= top && mid < bot) return i;
  }
  return 0;
}

function go(i) {
  i = Math.max(0, Math.min(slides.length - 1, i));
  slides[i].scrollIntoView({ behavior: 'smooth' });
}

document.addEventListener('keydown', (e) => {
  const i = currentIndex();
  switch (e.key) {
    case 'ArrowRight': case 'ArrowDown': case 'PageDown': case ' ':
      e.preventDefault(); go(i + 1); break;
    case 'ArrowLeft': case 'ArrowUp': case 'PageUp':
      e.preventDefault(); go(i - 1); break;
    case 'Home': e.preventDefault(); go(0); break;
    case 'End':  e.preventDefault(); go(slides.length - 1); break;
  }
});
</script>
